# seerr_network_settings (Resource)

Manage Seerr network settings via `/api/v1/settings/network`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "seerr_network_settings" "example" {
  trust_proxy            = true
  force_ipv4_first       = false
  api_request_timeout_ms = 15000

  dns_cache {
    enabled       = true
    force_min_ttl = 60
    force_max_ttl = 600
  }

  proxy {
    enabled                = true
    hostname               = "proxy.internal"
    port                   = 8080
    use_ssl                = false
    user                   = "proxy-user"
    password               = var.proxy_password
    bypass_filter          = "localhost,*.svc.cluster.local"
    bypass_local_addresses = true
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `api_request_timeout_ms` (Number) Maximum time in milliseconds Seerr waits for external API responses.
- `csrf_protection` (Boolean)
- `dns_cache` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--dns_cache))
- `force_ipv4_first` (Boolean)
- `proxy` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--proxy))
- `trust_proxy` (Boolean)

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

<a id="nestedblock--dns_cache"></a>
### Nested Schema for `dns_cache`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Boolean)
- `force_max_ttl` (Number)
- `force_min_ttl` (Number)


<a id="nestedblock--proxy"></a>
### Nested Schema for `proxy`

Optional:

- `bypass_filter` (String)
- `bypass_local_addresses` (Boolean)
- `enabled` (Boolean)
- `hostname` (String)
- `password` (String, Sensitive)
- `port` (Number)
- `use_ssl` (Boolean)
- `user` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import seerr_network_settings.example network
```
